title: Soft-delete cascade with has_many children that should also be soft-deleted
prompt: A User has_many Posts, and Posts have soft-delete (deleted_at) support. When a User is soft-deleted, all their Posts should also be soft-deleted (but NOT hard-deleted). Write: (1) User schema with deleted_at (lib/my_app/accounts/user.ex), (2) Post schema with deleted_at + belongs_to user (lib/my_app/blog/post.ex), (3) an MyApp.Accounts.soft_delete_user/1 function that uses Ecto.Multi to soft-delete the user AND all non-deleted posts in one transaction — using an update_all query for efficiency (lib/my_app/accounts.ex).
tier_rationale: Requires Ecto.Multi orchestration + update_all + soft-delete semantics + preventing hard deletes. Legendary because the coordination is non-obvious.
research_citation: Dashbit — Soft deletes with Ecto: 'error prone, especially once you consider foreign keys cascade and deletion rules.'
